body.title: Router: add domain/tier/outcome + per-ticket correlator to the per-call cost record
body.description:
**Problem:** the intention 'learn from it every single time' + Akien's validation ('prove on live tickets one at a time, watching actual cost, look at the logs on a ticket') require every dispatch to be greppable PER TICKET with its full routing+cost story. Today budget_ledger records {provider, model, input_tokens, output_tokens, cost_usd, ts} — so tokens+dollars exist, but domain, difficulty tier, outcome(DONE/fail), and a per-ticket correlator do NOT. Those fields are NEW (this design just invented domain+tier), so this is a real delta, not a hollow 'verify it exists'. **Proposed shape:** (1) read what budget_ledger + the routing crossing-log record today; (2) add domain, tier, outcome(DONE|fail|escalated), and a ticket correlator (ticket_id — thread it from DS through InferenceRequest, or via an existing field agent_id/coa_id/session_id if it already maps 1:1 to a ticket) to BOTH the routing crossing-log line AND the ledger row; (3) make a single call's full record locatable by ticket_id via grep. Reuse crossing-log/budget_ledger — NO new store, NO SQLite (Postgres/flat-file only). **Affected files:** devices/inference/device.py (crossing-log fields + correlator), devices/inference/budget_ledger.py (row columns), possibly devices/dicksimnel/ (thread ticket_id into the request). **Design rules:** NO SQLITE; flat-file/Postgres only; log at the crossing. **Scope boundary:** IN: add domain+tier+outcome+ticket-correlator to the per-call record; grep-locatable per ticket. OUT: dashboards, aggregation/reporting, auto-tuning. **Test plan (stub-first red->green):** (1) a dispatch writes a record containing domain, tier, source, tokens, dollars, outcome; (2) the record is locatable by ticket_id (grep); (3) a failed (non-DONE) call records outcome=fail WITH its cost. **Completion criteria:** every dispatch produces a per-ticket cost+outcome record including domain+tier+dollars+outcome, locatable per ticket by grep; tests green.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.result: Per-call cost+outcome record shipped: every dispatch emits a cost_record log (ticket_id, domain, tier, source, model, tokens, dollars, call_outcome) on all exit paths incl. failures; grep-locatable per ticket. InferenceRequest.ticket_id threaded from DS + preserved through all 3 in-dispatch rebuilds (also fixed dropped domain/pin_reason). DS worker_listener logs task_outcome (done|escalated|fail) keyed by ticket_id. 3 tests green; 0 regressions (330 passed). Ledger Postgres columns deferred to T-inference-ledger-domain-tier-columns (needs verified migration). Commit 15c98e12.
body.intention: I intend that every inference call records its domain, tier, source, cost and outcome per ticket, so we learn from every call and can watch the real cost on live tickets one at a time.
body.unproven_reason: Additive-log-record class — same lever now tracked as T-proof-emitter-additive-surface-mode: the 3 cost_record tests are real behavioral assertions but proof_emitter's diff-inversion strips the new test file (collateral, not red). Additionally, the FULL validation intention ('watch actual cost on live tickets, one at a time') is only provable by running live DS tickets against Hex and reading the cost_records — an operational proof this static test cannot stand in for.
